compartments.label: Lumen
compartments.snippet: Labeled top-left, region above the microvilli/brush border; contains bacteria and extracellular toxins.

compartments.label: Intestinal cell
compartments.snippet: Large box enclosing most nodes, labeled bottom-left; represents the C. elegans intestinal epithelial cell.

compartments.label: Nucleus
compartments.snippet: Large reddish oval inside the intestinal cell, labeled bottom-center; contains ESRE, cellular stress response genes, antimicrobial defence genes.

compartments.label: Mitochondria
compartments.snippet: Teal organelle, center-top inside intestinal cell; labeled; source of UPRmt, mtROS, and iron-status signals.

compartments.label: LRO stress / pH increase
compartments.snippet: Circular organelle on left side inside intestinal cell; represents lysosome-related organelle stress with pH increase; intermediate in Pyocyanin→p38 cascade.

genes.snippet: Left side of intestinal cell membrane, membrane protein glyph; also annotated as SARM1.
Yellow oval, annotated as MAPKKK; part of p38 MAPK cascade.
Yellow oval, annotated as MAPKK; downstream of NSY-1.
Yellow oval, annotated as p38 MAPK; downstream of SEK-1.
Blue membrane receptor, upper right of intestinal cell membrane; responds to extracellular signals.
Blue oval, top-center of intestinal cell near mitochondria; part of UPRmt branch.
Purple box, annotated as ATF-4; downstream of UPRmt/mitochondria and HSP-60.
Purple box, near mitochondria/iron region; regulates antimicrobial defence genes.
Purple oval; activated by mtROS; regulates nuclear gene expression.
Purple box, annotated as FOXO; transcription factor stack; target of PMK-1 and regulates cellular stress response genes.
Purple box, annotated as Nrf2; transcription factor stack; regulates cellular stress response genes.
Purple box; transcription factor stack; regulates cellular stress response genes; adjacent to ROS input.
Purple box, lower left; activated by PMK-1; drives antimicrobial defence genes.
Purple box, right side; activates ESRE in nucleus.
Purple box labeled CEBP-1/2, right side; activates ESRE.
Purple box labeled CEBP-1/2, right side; activates ESRE; listed together with CEBP-1.
Purple box, right side; activated downstream of ExoA/Translation inhibition; activates ESRE and antimicrobial defence genes; one connector may be a T-bar (flagged as uncertain).
Purple box, annotated as HNF-4, far right inside nucleus edge; activated by PCN; drives antimicrobial defence genes.
